frs_context.description: Enables use of RS232 CTS signal for flow control and/or scan control
frs_context.notes: When CTS flow control is enabled, CTS signal controls transmission of data to host. When disabled, scanner simply transmits to host regardless of any activity on CTS line. Scan control using CTS line can be enabled/disabled. When this option is enabled, CTS line must be active for scanner to read and transmit data. While CTS line is inactive, scanner remains in a host-disabled state (RS232_Xmit_Disable); following a successful label transmission, CTS signal must transition to inactive and then to active to enable scanning for next label. Magsl CTS scan control works the same as CTS scan control except the CTS toggle at the end of transmission is not required, and the scanner will therefore not be disabled waiting for the CTS toggle.
frs_context.head: USB-COM Parameters

frs_context.description: Enables RS232 flow control using XON/XOFF characters

frs_context.description: Enables ability of scanner to support RS232 ACK/NAK protocol

frs_context.description: Specifies ASCII character to be used as ACK character (default 06hex)

frs_context.description: Specifies ASCII character to be used as NAK character (default 15hex)

frs_context.description: Specifies good-read beep duration
frs_context.notes: Times have a tolerance of plus/minus 25%. In normal usage the good read beep length should always be set higher than 01. Due to the way the software handles the timer the actual timer value (when set to 01) is between 0 mS and 10mS. A settings of 01 is generally used for testing purposes only.

frs_context.notes: Mode 2 applies to RS232 STD and RS232 WN only. If set to mode 2 in other interfaces, mode 0 will be assumed. This setting will only work if CTS Scan control is enabled. Mode3 - When beeping after each output structure decoded, if multiple output structures, there is a delay after the beep has finished. The delay is equal to the good beep length or can be set by a ULE script.

Disabled=illumination is not controlled by host enable/disable commands, illumination stays on when disabled Enabled=illumination is controlled by host enable/disable commands, illumination is on when enabled and off when disabled
frs_context.head: Illumination

frs_context.description: Enables illumination banks
frs_context.notes: A bit setting of "1" equals illumination enabled, a bit setting of "0" equals illumination disabled. Bit 0 = Red enable Bit 1 = Reserved Bit 2 = Reserved Bit 3 = Reserved Bit 4 = Reserved Bit 5 = Reserved Bit 6 = Reserved Bit 7 = Reserved
frs_context.head: Illumination

CI_ILLUMINATION_RED_BRIGHT_DURATION.context: Red Bank Bright Duration in microseconds

frs_context.parameter: Red Bank Bright Duration in microseconds
frs_context.description: Controls brightness of red bright illumination via duration value.
frs_context.notes: The FPGA register is 16 bits, but the max allowed value at this time is 200 usec for the red LED banks. This value must be greater than or equal to CI_ILLUMINATION_RED_DIM_DURATION (tag 0703).
frs_context.head: Illumination

frs_context.description: Controls brightness of red dim illumination via duration value.
frs_context.notes: The FPGA register is 16 bits, but the max allowed value at this time is 200 usec for the red LED banks. This value must be less than or equal to CI_ILLUMINATION_RED_BRIGHT_DURATION (tag 0701).
frs_context.head: Illumination

frs_context.description: Specifies the number of entries available in the ProofedLabelHistoryTable. This table is a list of recent ProofedLabels that is used for double read protection.
frs_context.head: Double Read Control

frs_context.description: Specifies minimum allowable time between consecutive good reads of same 1D(linearcode)label
frs_context.notes: Setting CI_DOUBLE_READ_TIMEOUT to a value 05 or below will result in a loss of accuracy on P2 and P5 Addon Labels.
frs_context.head: Double Read Control

frs_context.description: Minimum allowable time between consecutive good reads of same label for 2D labels.
frs_context.head: Double Read Control

frs_context.description: The time segments of a decoded label are required to be absent from the volume.
frs_context.notes: Specifies the number of frames or 25msec cycles that the segments are stored by the software before being discarded if a label has not been successfully decoded during the current label in volume session. The "Label in volume session" is defined as the number of cycles between when the label gone time is first started until the label gone cycle timer expires. The maximum amount of time before the label gone frame timer expiration processing takes place can be the configured number of cycles + 1. In normal usage the label gone cycle timer should always be set higher than 01. Due to the way the software handles the timer the actual timer value (when set to 1) is between 0 cycles and 1 cycle. Settings of 0 and 1 are generally used for testing purposes only.
frs_context.head: Double Read Control

frs_context.description: Minimum allowable time between consecutive good reads of same GS1 DataBar-14/Omnidirectional label.
frs_context.head: Double Read Control

frs_context.description: Allows the user to configure the scanner (volume) button to different modes of operation.
frs_context.notes: The button will still control handheld state operations even if this tag is set to 03. Handheld state button operations are purely controlled by CI_HANDHELDSTATE_ENABLE.
frs_context.head: Buttons

frs_context.description: Timer duration for the delay disable feature. If set to 0, the delay disable feature is disabled.
frs_context.head: Disable Control

frs_context.description: Enables the Handheld State of operation
frs_context.notes: 00=Disable 01=Manual When in Manual Mode the setting 01 and larger is also used as the HandheldStateTimer setting multiplied by 100ms. Ex: a setting of 04 = 400ms for the HandheldStateTimer.
frs_context.head: Handheld State

frs_context.description: Enables or Disables CenterZoneLabelProofing
frs_context.notes: In "Always On" mode, Zone Proofing will be applied to labels presented to the scanner while in Active State and in HandHeld State. Depending on the size of the center zone set in CI_CENTER_ZONE_SIZE, this may drastically diminish reading performance in Active State. NOTE: Labels rendered by segment assembly carry no coordinate data, and will not be proofed if CenterZoneLabelProofing is enabled.
frs_context.head: Handheld State

frs_context.description: Specifies the scalar that defines the CenterZoneLabelProofing zone size as a percent of the full image.
frs_context.notes: Scalar is decimal percent and applied to both X and Y axes. Pixel fractions round up.

frs_context.head: Green Spot

frs_context.description: Used to control the method of processing coupon labels.
frs_context.notes: UPC/EAN labels that start with a '5' are defined as coupon labels. RSS Expanded labels that start with '8110' are defined as coupon labels. When this configuration item is set to 1, UPC/EAN coupon labels will decode but RSS/DataBar Expanded coupon labels will not. RSS/DataBar Expanded labels that are not coupon labels will decode and all UPC/EAN labels will decode. When this configuration items is set to 2, RSS/DataBar Expanded coupon labels will decode but .UPC/EAN coupon labels will not. UPC/EAN labels that are not coupon labels will decode and all RSS/DataBar Expanded labels will decode. When this configuration item is set to 3, UPC/EAN coupon labels will be given priority. When this configuration item is set to 4, DataBar coupon labels will be given priority.NOTE:
frs_context.head: Code Selection

frs_context.description: Sets the duration of the UPCA / DataBar coupon label priority timer.
frs_context.notes: This configuration item is used to set the initial value of the coupon label priority timer. Each increment of the configuration item represents 10 milliseconds.
frs_context.head: Code Selection

frs_context.notes: Used in Curie with EVL to control 1D decoding timeout
frs_context.head: Code Selection

frs_context.description: 2D Vision Library decoder dwelling time.

frs_context.head: Code Selection

frs_context.description: Controls bar code margin requirements for decoding. Byte[0]: Bit0: 1=TRUE, 0=FALSE. VL_PROPERTY_CODABAR_SHORT_MARGIN Bit1: 1=TRUE, 0=FALSE. VL_PROPERTY_I25_SHORT_MARGIN Bit2: 1=TRUE, 0=FALSE. VL_PROPERTY_C93_SHORT_MARGIN Bit3: 1=TRUE, 0=FALSE.VL_PROPERTY_C128_SHORT_MARGIN Bit4: 1=TRUE, 0=FALSE. VL_PROPERTY_C39_SHORT_MARGIN Bit5: 1=TRUE, 0=FALSE. VL_PROPERTY_EAN_SHORT_MARGIN (if bytes[1]-[2] == 0) Bit6: 1=TRUE, 0=FALSE. VL_PROPERTY_MSI_SHORT_MARGIN Bit7: 1=TRUE, 0=FALSE. VL_PROPERTY_25IND_SHORT_MARGIN Byte[1]-[2]: 16 bit value VL_PROPERTY_EAN_CODE_QUIET_ZONE Byte[3]-[4]: 16 bit value VL_PROPERTY_EAN_ADDON_QUIET_ZONE
frs_context.notes: Consult Vision Library documentation for more information on these properties.

frs_context.head: Code Selection

frs_context.description: This configuration item is used to enable 1D inverted label reading.
frs_context.notes: The meaning of the enumerated values has changed. There may be a minor performance decrease depending on the symbology.

frs_context.description: Controls the method of reading inverse 2D symbologies.
frs_context.notes: The meaning of the enumerated values has changed. There may be a minor performance decrease depending on the symbology. Applies to the following 2D matrix symbologies: Datamatrix and Dotcode. Additional matrix symbologies may be added by the VL platform team.
frs_context.head: Code Selection

frs_context.description: The number of additional required reads when more than one string of label data is decoded during a session to read a single barcode.
frs_context.notes: This configuration value is added to the symbology specific minimum number of reads required when more than one string of Label data is decoded during a session to read a single barcode. When more than one string is detected, the required reads Becomes the sum of the configuration item value and the symbology specific minimum read setting. This helps to increase the Data security when there are multiple results while attempting to decode of a poor quality label.
frs_context.head: Code Selection

frs_context.description: Controls the Vision Library minimum read control setting for numerous symbologies.
frs_context.notes: Overrides the default minimum reads behavior. The first three bytes are used to enable frame based minimum reads. Each of the bits is used to indicate if the related CI_XXX_MINIMUM_READ item uses frame based minimum reads. BYTE 0 (left most byte): Bit 0 = UPCA Bit 1 = UPCE Bit 2 = EAN8 Bit 3 = EAN13 Bit 4 = not used (P2_ADDON VSL only) Bit 5 = not used (P5_ADDON VSL only) Bit 6 = INSTORE Bit 7 = EAN_TWO_LABEL BYTE 1: Bit 0 = COD128 Bit 1 = CODE39 Bit 2 = CODE93 Bit 3 = CODABAR Bit 4 = I2OF5 Bit 5 = S25 Bit 6 = MSI Bit 7 = reserved BYTE 2: Bit 0 = DATABAR_14 Bit 1 = DATABAR_EXPANDED Bit 2 thru Bit 7 = reserved Modify minimum read behavior for specific data patterns. BYTE 3: Bit 0 = MINIMUM_READ+1 for special patterns Bit 1 = MINIMUM_READ+1 for UPCEAN ambiguous characters Bit 2 thru Bit 7 = reserved
frs_context.head: Code Selection

frs_context.description: Enables optional 2 Digit Supplemental add-ons.
frs_context.notes: If a UPC/EAN base label and a 2 Digit Supplemental Addon are both decoded, the scanner will transmit the base label and addon. If a UPC/EAN base label is decoded without an addon, the base label will be transmitted without an addon. Please note that conditional addons settings (CI_CONDITIONAL_ADDON...) are considered by the scanner before optional addon settings.
frs_context.head: Add-On Configuration

frs_context.description: Enables optional 5 Digit Supplemental add-ons.
frs_context.notes: If a UPC/EAN base label and a 5 Digit Supplemental Addon are both decoded, the scanner will transmit the base label and addon. If a UPC/EAN base label is decoded without an addon, the base label will be transmitted without an addon. Please note that conditional addons settings (CI_CONDITIONAL_ADDON...) are considered by the scanner before optional addon settings.
frs_context.head: Add-On Configuration

frs_context.head: Add-On Configuration

frs_context.description: Enables conversion of UPCE, UPCA, EAN8, and EAN13 labels into 14 character GTIN.
frs_context.notes: If add-on information is present on the base label prior to the conversion taking place, the addon information will be appended to the converted GTIN label.
frs_context.head: EAN/UPC Global Settings

frs_context.description: Enables Composites for the UPC/EAN families of labels
frs_context.notes: A setting of 0 (zero) disables this feature. Bit 0 - if set enables Composites for UPCE If clear disables Composites for UPCE Bit 1 - if set enables Composites for UPCA If clear disables Composites for UPCA Bit 2 - if set enables Composites for EAN8 If clear disables Composites for EAN8 Bit 3 - if set enables Composites for EAN13 If clear disables Composites for EAN13
frs_context.head: EAN/UPC Global Settings

frs_context.description: Set the timer value for the UPC/EAN composite timer timeout
frs_context.notes: This timer is used to delay the base label until the composite arrives. If the timer expires prior to composite being decoded, only the base label will be decoded
frs_context.head: EAN/UPC Global Settings

frs_context.description: Enable UPC/EAN character correlation.

frs_context.description: Enables calculation and verification of price/weight check digits
frs_context.notes: Applies to all UPCA labels with a number-system character of 2 and EAN/JAN13 labels with a Flag1 digit of 2 The 03 and 04 options are for legacy European (EAN/JAN13) behavior only.
frs_context.head: EAN/UPC Global Settings

frs_context.description: Allows the user to utilize a specific set of decoding features for EAN8
frs_context.notes: As the decoding level is set higher (I.e larger number) the decoders become more aggressive (also, the likelihood of misreads increases). 0 - allows tag item control defined by CI_EAN8_GUARD_INSERTION, CI_EAN8_STITCH_EXACT_LABEL_HALVES, CI_EAN8_STITCH_UNLIKE_LABEL_HALVES [1-4] ignores settings of these tags 1 - allows decoding from center of label outward 2 - allows end guard insertion similar to CI_EAN8_GUARD_INSERTION 3 - allows stitching with no overlap similar to CI_EAN8_STITCH_EXACT_LABEL_HALVES 4 - allows stitching of halves with overlap similar to CI_EAN8_STITCH_UNLIKE_LABEL_HALVES
frs_context.head: EAN/UPC Global Settings

frs_context.description: Allows the user to utilize a specific set of decoding features for UPCA / EAN13.
frs_context.notes: As the decoding level is set higher (I.e larger number) the decoders become more aggressive (also, the likelihood of misreads increases). 0 - allows tag item control defined by CI_UPCEAN_GUARD_INSERTION, CI_UPCEAN_STITCH_EXACT_LABEL_HALVES, CI_UPCEAN_STITCH_UNLIKE_LABEL_HALVES [1-4] ignores settings of these tags 1 - allows decoding from center of label outward 2 - allows end guard insertion similar to CI_UPCEAN_GUARD_INSERTION 3 - allows stitching with no overlap similar to CI_UPCEAN_STITCH_EXACT_LABEL_HALVES 4 - allows stitching of halves with overlap similar to CI_UPCEAN_STITCH_UNLIKE_LABEL_HALVES
frs_context.head: EAN/UPC Global Settings

CI_GS1_DIGITAL_LINK_SET_TRANSMIT_MODE.context: GS1 Digital Link Set Transmit Mode

frs_context.parameter: GS1 Digital Link Set Transmit Mode
frs_context.description: GS1 Digital Link Set Transmit Mode
frs_context.notes: 00=Disabled, do not look for GS1 Label Set 01=Send first decoded label of the GS1 Label Set, discard other labels of the set read within the timeout specified by CI_GS1_DIGITAL_LINK_SET_TIMEOUT 03=Give priority to GS1 2D Label, if 2D label not seen within time specified by CI_GS1_DIGITAL_LINK_SET_TIMEOUT, send the DWM or 1D label if read. If both DWM and 1D labels read, the DWM label will be transmitted. 02=Send all decoded labels decoded and prepend label data with Label Set Number [USBOEM interface transmits labels as unknown label type]
frs_context.head: GS1 Digital Link

frs_context.description: Formatting options for GS1 2D labels
frs_context.notes: For conversion options 03 and 04, the simplest (smallest) of the listed symbologies that can completely contain the GTIN data will be selected as the conversion target. For conversion option 03, even if the label is compatible with EAN8, it will be sent as EAN13 and even if the label is compatible with UPCE, it will be sent as UPCA. 1D labels will be sent in their original label format.
frs_context.head: GS1 Digital Link

frs_context.description: Time to monitor for GS1 Digital Link set labels.
frs_context.head: GS1 Digital Link

CI_GS1_DIGITAL_LINK_SET_DOUBLE_READ_TIME.context: GS1 Digital Link Double Read Timeout

frs_context.parameter: GS1 Digital Link Double Read Timeout
frs_context.description: Specifies minimum allowable time between consecutive reads of the same GS1 Digital Link Set. Any labels seen after timeout specified by CI_GS1_DIGITAL_LINK_SET_TIMEOUT has expired will not be transmitted until after this timeout has expired.
frs_context.notes: In normal usage the double read timer should always be set higher than 01. Due to the way the software handles the timer the actual timer value (when set to 1) is between 0 mS and 5mS. A settings of 1 is generally used for testing purposes only.
frs_context.head: GS1 Digital Link

CI_GS1_DIGITAL_LINK_LABEL_SET_ID.context: GS1 Digital Link Label Set ID

frs_context.parameter: GS1 Digital Link Label Set ID
frs_context.description: Field identifier for the Label Set Number that is transmitted before the label data
frs_context.notes: The label ID is 4 bytes. The first 3 bytes are characters for the label ID. A 00 (hex) value in the first 3 bytes indicates the end of the label ID characters. Only active when CI_GS1_DIGITAL_LINK_SET_TRANSMIT_MODE = 02, Send all labels.
frs_context.head: GS1 Digital Link

frs_context.notes: There are 8 characters in this conversion string: Characters [1] [2] [3] [4] [5] [6] [7] [8]. The following conversions occur: Character [1] is converted to Character [2]; If Character [1] is 0xFF, there is no conversion. Character [3] is converted to Character [4]; If Character [3] is 0xFF, there is no conversion. Character [5] is converted to Character [6]; If Character [5] is 0xFF, there is no conversion. Character [7] is converted to Character [8]; If Character [7] is 0xFF, there is no conversion.

frs_context.notes: Coordinate values must follow these rules: 0 <= xmin < 1280 0 <= xmax < 1280 Xmin < xmax 0 <= ymin < 800 0 <= ymax < 800 Ymin < ymax If the xmax/ymax values are configured larger than the maximum values above, they will default to 1 less than their respective maximum values. frs_context.description: Special test data to be used by development .. defined by product
frs_context.notes: Only to be set by engineering. Reserved
frs_context.head: Developer

frs_context.description: This configuration items allows certain testing features to be enabled or disabled.
frs_context.notes: This allows the software to be used by SW test to control certain operation in the scanner. The default value of this configuration item as built is zero. Bit 0 : Disable DoubleRead Bit 1: Disable UI Heartbeat Bit 2 : Enable EVL/VL/LC file logging Bit 4 : Enable imager errors Bit 5 : 1= UI Heartbeat @ 10Hz Bit 6 : 1= Statistics update every 10 sec, button debounce 20ms
frs_context.head: Developer

frs_context.description: Config item to allow software development to put device under test into specific mode.
frs_context.notes: NOTE: This configuration item is for SW development only and should not be tested by SW Test Bit 0 - IPE debugging Toggle Bit 1 - Imager config Test Toggle (on by default) Bit 04 = Use COCO channel instead of ipe_control Bit 7 - Reserved
frs_context.head: Developer
